title: BLKANDNBLK not reported on arrays so assignment statement does not take effect.

features.name: combinatorial_array_read_with_nonblocking_write_same_element
features.description: Code should include an array element read inside a combinatorial always @(*) block, where the same array element is written with non-blocking assignment (<=) in a sequential always @(posedge) block. This triggers Verilator BLKANDNBLK false positive causing the combinatorial assignment to be silently deleted.

features.name: combinational_block_reading_array_written_by_sequential_block
features.description: Code should include a combinatorial always block reading an array element that is also assigned in a sequential always block, where the read is used to drive an output signal. Verilator may incorrectly schedule this as a delete-domain assignment.
